title: SQL Injection in product search
evidence: GET /search?q=' OR '1'='1 returned all fake product records during a controlled assessment. Tester contact: web-audit@example.test
impact: Attackers may read or modify application data if untrusted input reaches database queries.
recommendation: Use parameterized queries; validate input server-side; add regression tests for search query handling.

title: Cross-Site Scripting in feedback form
evidence: Submitted <script>alert('auditforge')</script> and the value was reflected without output encoding.
impact: Attackers may execute JavaScript in another user's browser and perform actions as that user.
recommendation: Encode output by context; sanitize rich text fields; add tests for reflected and stored input.

title: Missing Security Headers
evidence: Response did not include Content-Security-Policy or X-Frame-Options headers in the fake test environment.
impact: Missing browser security headers can increase exposure to clickjacking and script injection impact.
recommendation: Add a baseline Content-Security-Policy; set frame-ancestors or X-Frame-Options; review other defensive headers.

title: Directory Listing Enabled
evidence: The uploads directory returned an index page with fake filenames such as report-placeholder.pdf and avatar-sample.png.
impact: Directory metadata may reveal application structure or help attackers enumerate uploaded files.
recommendation: Disable directory indexes and serve only explicitly authorized static files.

title: Weak TLS Configuration
evidence: The test endpoint accepted TLS 1.0 during a controlled TLS configuration check using fake hostnames only.
impact: Legacy TLS support increases downgrade and compliance risk.
recommendation: Disable TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1; require TLS 1.2 or later with modern cipher suites.
